_comment: Example passport using v0.2 granular permission scopes. Contrast with free-tool.json (v0.1 boolean permissions). This is a GitHub file-search MCP tool with scoped permissions instead of broad boolean flags.
capabilities: Search files in GitHub repositories by content
Return file path, line number, and matching excerpt
Support regex and plain-text search modes
Paginate results up to 100 matches per call

network.notes: Only contacts api.github.com. No other network access. Uses GitHub REST API v3 search endpoint.

memory.notes: No memory access. Stateless per call.

api.notes: Caller provides GitHub personal access token via GITHUB_TOKEN env var or X-GitHub-Token header. Token is never logged or persisted.

permission_manifest.notes: This tool is intentionally scoped to api.github.com only. It performs read-only search operations. No data is stored between calls.

data_handling.notes: No data is retained after a call. Search queries and results pass through memory only.

tools.name: search_files
tools.description: Search for text or regex patterns across files in a GitHub repository

risk_summary.ai_generated_notes: This tool contacts api.github.com exclusively over HTTPS. Caller-provided GitHub tokens are not logged. No file system, terminal, or wallet access. Suitable for use in automated pipelines without human-in-the-loop.
human_reviewed_findings: Confirmed: only api.github.com in network scope
Confirmed: no credential logging in source
Note: rate limits depend on caller's GitHub token tier — shared tokens may exhaust quickly in high-volume pipelines

review_history.notes: Creator confirmed identity and metadata accuracy via GitHub OAuth
3 community reviewers confirmed network scope and credential handling. No issues raised.
